pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 70: 'int discountPercent = 5; return subtotal * (discountPercent / 100);' - Bug comment at line 69: 'BUG 1: Integer division. 5/100 is 0.'
setup_task.sh line 78: 'if (subtotal > 100.00)' with comment at line 77: 'BUG 2: Exact boundary error. Should be >= 100.00'
setup_task.sh lines 94-96: case 'NJ': with comment 'BUG 3: Missing break! Falls through to Default' - no break statement before default case

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic Maven project (EcommerceBackend) with three deliberately introduced logic bugs in the OrderService class: (1) integer division bug where 5/100 with integer operands yields 0 instead of 0.05, causing discount to be 0%; (2) boundary condition error using > 100.00 instead of >= 100.00 for shipping threshold; (3) switch fallthrough in tax calculation where the NJ case is missing a break statement, causing it to apply the default 5% tax rate instead of the 6.6% NJ rate. Test cases validate these bugs with specific input values (orders of $1000, $600, $100, $50 with states TX, NJ, NV, XX) and expect specific calculated totals ($997.5, $598.5, $105.0, $106.60, $63.29). All project code is synthetically generated by setup_task.sh (lines 14-104), not from any external source. Hidden tests are injected at verification time to check if all three bugs are fixed.
